[00:00–00:30]
The meeting has been duly called and the notice for this meeting has been posted in accordance with
the Texas Open Meetings Act, Texas Government Code Chapter 551.041. The first item is public comment
on agenda items. At board meetings, public comment time is set aside for individuals who wish to
address the board during special meetings, workshops, and retreats, comments regarding agenda items.
While at regular meetings, individuals can comment on both agenda and non agenda items.

[00:30–01:00]
Okay. During special meetings, workshops and retreats, comments regarding agenda items, while at
regular meetings, individuals can comment on both items. Individuals who wish to participate during
the portion of the meeting designated for public comment shall sign up by completing the appropriate
form on the district website, by submitting the form in person at the ad building, or by sending an
email with the district form as an attachment to the email address listed on the form.

[01:00–01:30]
Public comment forms must be received no later than noon on the day of the meeting and shall
indicate the agenda item or topic on which they wish to address the board. The length of public
comment shall be at the discretion of the board president, not to exceed four minutes. We will do
two minutes and 30 seconds today. Because individual rights are protected by various laws and board
policies, complaints regarding specific employees or students should be directed to the
administration. If a person makes complaints about a specific employee or student during public
comment, the board president has the discretion.

[01:30–02:00]
to move the board into closed session to hear such complaints. If anyone makes a complaint about a
specific employee by name or job title, the board may hold the public comment until the end of the
public comments and hear all such complaints in closed session. Under the law, the board cannot
deliberate, discuss, or make a decision on any subject that is not on the agenda. So as a general
matter, the board does not respond to questions at public comment. Specific factual information or
review of existing policy, however, were legally allowed, be provided in response.

[02:00–02:30]
to inquiries or questions. At this time we will begin public comment, and our first speaker is
Jennifer David Owl. We ask that you come forward to the microphone. Please state your name, address,
and the agenda item number which you are speaking about. Good evening. I am Jennifer David Al and I
live in the district.

[02:30–03:00]
Given the ruling that came out a week ago. The answer is to follow Judge Lake's order and don't

understands, an appeal is not a do over. There will be a lot of deference given to Judge Lake in
terms of the factual findings that he made.

[03:00–03:30]
It's not a brand new trial at the Court of Appeals. The question is going to be whether his fact

Reagan. He is consistently conservative. The Fifth Circuit is consistently conservative. They will
grade his paper with a light pen. And I will tell you, having gotten through most of the paper, it
is bulletproof. It's meticulous, it's detailed. He understood the importance of the case.

[05:00–05:30]
This is simply about how the board is elected and not an indictment of the district. Thank you.

[14:00–14:30]
Secondly, this district has claimed that we were in a fiscal crisis as of last year. This rationale

[14:30–15:00]
This is your job. You all campaigned as conservatives. I expect you to be fiscally responsible and

by more affluent areas to dominate elections, often at the expense of candidates who would better
